Money Stuff =0A Imagine how you would solve this problem from first princip=
les. It is October =0A2022, and two big supermarket companies, Albertsons C=
os. and K =0A=0A=0A =0A<https://www.bloomberg.com/opinion/authors/ARbTQlRLR=
jE/matthew-s-levine=3Fcmpid=3DBBD121724_MONEYSTUFF&utm_medium=3Demail&utm_s=
ource=3Dnewsletter&utm_term=3D241217&utm_campaign=3Dmoneystuff>=0A =0A<http=
s://sli.bloomberg.com/click=3Fs=3D825881&stpe=3Ddefault&li=3D12058042&m=3D2=
1d6dea82d6b16615cf44233be620525&p=3D12172024>=0A=0A=0A=0ASupermarkets=0A=0A=
Imagine how you would solve this problem from first principles. It is Octob=
er =0A2022, and two big supermarket companies, Albertsons Cos. and Kroger C=
o., are =0Adiscussing a merger. Both companies feel the need to get bigger =
to compete with =0AWalmart, Target, Costco and Amazon, and this seems like =
a logical combination. =0AKroger is the bigger company, with roughly twice =
as much revenue, so naturally =0AKroger will buy Albertsons for cash.=0A=0A=
There is a problem. The problem is that the US Federal Trade Commission, in=
 =0A2022, is skeptical of big mergers, and this would be a big merger. In =

se that it is not all that convenient for a SpaceX employee to sell 15% =0A=
of her stock: =E2=80=9CAt SpaceX, staff have regularly had the option of se=

biannually,=E2=80=9D but =0Amaybe you want to renovate your kitchen between=

sure to your employer in the form of your paycheck =0Aand human capital, so=

ents,=E2=80=9D and those who believe =E2=80=9Cyou should have roughly 300% =
of =0Ayour net worth in your employer=E2=80=99s stock, because your employe=
r is awesome and =0Ayou should be all in.=E2=80=9D I am, by nature, very mu=
ch a 0% guy.=0A=0AOne gets the sense that everyone at SpaceX is more of a 3=
00% person. Selling =0A15% of your stock would mean owning slightly less Sp=
aceX stock, and one of the =0Amain benefits of working at SpaceX is that yo=
u get to own SpaceX stock.Borrowing=0A15% of the value of your SpaceX stock=

 Obviously it costs you 9%, and you=E2=80=99ll feel bad =0Aabout it if Spac=

=0Astock might go down then maybe you shouldn=E2=80=99t work at SpaceX.=0A=

ous institutions. It used to =0Abe that a private equity firm was a handful=
